question: How long do Indians live?

theme.label: Health and survival
readerQuestions: What is the survival or health outcome?
How much has it changed over time?
Is the measure about death, illness, or healthy years?
What does the national average hide?

plannedCharts.title: Life expectancy at birth, by sex
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The single clearest number for how long Indians live. Three lines — male, female, both sexes — from 2000 to 2030, with the UN's median projection visible after 2024.

plannedCharts.title: India vs the world
plannedCharts.subtitle: World Bank · 1960 to latest · life expectancy at birth
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: India's life expectancy in the context of China, the United States, and the world average. The convergence and the gap are both visible.

plannedCharts.title: Healthy life expectancy at birth
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Years lived are one thing. Years lived in full health — without disease or disability — is what people actually experience.

plannedCharts.title: Remaining life expectancy at age 60
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Life expectancy at birth is dominated by child mortality. Survival at 60 measures health in later life, where the next gains must come from.

plannedCharts.title: Life expectancy since 1960
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Zoomed out to six decades, the transformation is stark: Indian life expectancy has nearly doubled, from 46 to 72 years.

plannedCharts.title: Under-five child mortality rate
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Falling child deaths are the main engine that pushed Indian life expectancy from 46 to 72. Every child who survives to five adds a full lifetime.

plannedCharts.title: Infant mortality rate
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The first year of life is the single riskiest. Infant deaths are the most dependent on institutional healthcare — skilled birth attendants, neonatal units, immunisation.

plannedCharts.title: Health expenditure as a share of GDP
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Life expectancy is not just biology. It is also a function of what a country spends on health. This provides that structural context.

latestRows.Comments: The UN IGME estimates are not the official statistics of India. The Sample Registration System (SRS) of ORGI is the official data source of India for all mortality estimates.

sourceSummaries.title: Current health expenditure as share of GDP

selectionRules: Start from the page question, not from the indicator name.
Choose the smallest set of visuals that explains scale, trend, structure, comparison, and caveat.
Prefer mobile-friendly charts: line charts with sparse ticks, horizontal bars, share strips, pyramids only when essential, and no dense tables.
Use one strong pull quote only when the evidence supports it with a number.
Add glossary blocks for technical terms that normal readers may not know.
caveats: Use only figures present in lockedNumbers or sourceSummaries.
If a related theme indicator is present, you may use it for context, but do not pretend it is the main answer.
If source coverage is thin, say so directly.
Do not compare states, districts, or cities unless the evidence packet contains those geographies.
forbiddenClaims: Do not infer causality from a trend unless the evidence explicitly supports it.
Do not cite a source that is not present in sourceSummaries.
